titleStmt.title: SII 38.410: original edition by S. Swaminathan
No. 410. (A.R. No. 410 of 1912.) MARUDĀḌU, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT. On the west base, central shrine, Purandarēśvara temple.

p.hi: King: Rājanārāyaṇan Sambhuvarāyar (II) Year 16, Vaikāśi, 1354 A.D.

summary.p: This epigraph records that during the sixth regnal year of the above ruler (1344 A.D.) the tank of the village, Marudāḍu alias Vikramachōḻanallūr in Marudāḍu-nāḍu in Veṇkuṉṟa-kkōṭṭam (a sub-division) of Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam, got breached because of heavy floods caused by excessive rain. Consequently the sluice (kaliṅgu), which regulated the excess water, was damaged and water flow to the nearby villages, such as Koṭṭaiūr was stopped and it continued for nearly a decade. During the 16th regnal year (of the king) efforts were made to supply water to Kōttaiūr by excavating channels and raising the level of the embankment. Accordingly the ūrār of Kōṭṭaiūr entered into an agreement with the ūrār of Marudāḍu and obtained the right to dig channels by remitting 260 aṉrāḍu-naṟpaṇam. This inscription also details how these channels were dug and the course of the flow of the water to different tanks.
The account (of Marudāḍu) Malaikiṉiyaniṉṟāṉ Karikālachōḻa Brahmārāyaṉ and others figure as signatories.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . cakalalokac cakkiravatti Irācanārāyaṇan campuvarāyaṟku yāṇṭu 10 6vatu vaikācimātattu Oruṉāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattu veṇkuṉṟak koṭṭattu vātavūrṉāṭṭu koṭṭai Ūravaṟku Immaṇṭalattu Ik
koṭṭattu marutāṭunāṭṭu marutāṭāṉa vikkiramacoḻanallūr Ūravarom kālvilai piṟamāṇam paṇṇikkuṭutta paricāvatu Eṅkaḷūr Eri muṉṉāḷ Āṟāvatu peruveḷḷattile Uṭaintu Iṉṉāḷvaraiyum Aṭaikka mutalillāmaṟkkiṭakkaiyil Iv
veri Aṭaikka Eṅkaḷ periya Eriyil kaliṅku peruveḷḷattile puraṇṭa nirkoṭṭai Erikke pokak kālkāṇak kaṭavarkaḷākavum kāl kāṇumiṭattu Ilāvatteri Etir vāyāle poyaṉṟaleriyile pukuntu kiḻakaṭaiyāle poyk kāvirippākkattil Eri Etir vāyāle niṟpeṟavarakkile
koṇṭu pokki kālukkum karaikkum Emmilicainta Aṉṟāṭu naṭakkum naṟpaṇam 2 100 6 10 Ippaṇam Irunūṟṟaṟupatukkum kālukkum karaikkum vilai Āvatāka celakoṇṭu poruḷ māvaruti poruḷcelavolai Āvatākavum Ituvallaveṉṟu poruḷmāvaṟuti (poruḷ māvaruti) poruṭcilavolai kāṭṭeṟṟu Eṉṟu collap peṟātākavum Ip
ppaṭi cammatittu Irukālāvatu mukkālāvatum kālvilai piṟamāṇamThe two letters ṇam written below the line. paṇṇik kuṭuttom koṭṭai Ūravaṟku marutāṭṭu Ūrava(ra)rom Ippaṭi kkuc camainta Ūrkkaṇakku malaikiṉiyaniṉṟāṉ karikālacoḻappiramārāyaṉ Eḻuttu Ippaṭiaṟiveṉ Ivvūru taṭṭakaṉ Āṉai
muvataraiyaṉeṉ IppaṭuRead ippaṭi one letter pa is engraved below.Aṟiveṉ IThe letter ‘i’ is engraved below.vvūr nampiyāṉaiḻakiyāṉeṉ Ippaṭi Aṟiveṉ vātavūr cevveṉṉai vaccanantitevaṉ paḻanThe letter ‘n’ is engraved below.tivaṉeṉ Ippaṭi Aṟiveṉ vātavūr koṟṟūruṭaiyāṉ teva ko m perumāḷeṉ Ippaṭi AṟiveṉThe letter ‘ṉ’ is engraved below.cāThe medial letter � is engraved below.lai peṭṭu mātaṉ taṉtiramaṟu mārpaṉ kurukularāyaṉeṉ Ippaṭi Aṟiveṉ koṭuṅkālūr marutaThe letter ‘ta’ is engraved below.ttūruṭaiyāṉ
ṭirikālaRead ‘karikāla’ Two more lines are found in the same stone. But it is not clear whether it is connected with the above inscription. 1 i[ṉ*]ṉāya nār koyil nampiyā •••••••••• 2 piṭaiyaṉūrkku ik kālil ••••••••••coḻattaraiyaneṉ .
Digital edition of SII 38.410 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
